front: Film: Ethan Edwards (John Wayne) obsessively seeks niece Debbie, captured by Comanche; companion Martin Pawley (Jeffrey Hunter); Ethan says 'That'll be the day'; film begins and ends with Ethan framed in a cabin doorway — he turns and walks away at the end

Film: 'When the legend becomes fact, print the legend'; Tom Doniphon (John Wayne, calls Stewart's character 'pilgrim') is the true killer; senator Ranse Stoddard (Jimmy Stewart) begins by returning for Tom's funeral; black character Pompey recites the Declaration of Independence before a Lincoln portrait
back: The Man Who Shot Liberty Valance (John Ford, 1962)
work: The Man Who Shot Liberty Valance (1962)

front: Film: Ford's first Monument Valley film and first Wayne collaboration; Apaches chase the title vehicle; Wayne's introduction — shot goes out of focus as he twirls a rifle; film's negative is lost, extant releases use positive print found by Bogdanovich in Wayne's garage

Film: Sean Thornton (John Wayne), Irish-American boxer who accidentally killed an opponent, returns to Ireland; climax is a ridiculously long fistfight with Will Danaher (Victor McLaglen) from countryside to Cohan's Bar; inspired a long fight in John Carpenter's They Live

Director: called his style 'invisible technique'; Monument Valley backdrop for Stagecoach, The Searchers, My Darling Clementine; worked with Gregg Toland (deep focus) on The Grapes of Wrath; Andrew Sarris placed him in auteur theory 'pantheon'; Cahiers du Cinema analyzed his Young Mr. Lincoln
